title: Efficiency and market failure
paperName: Cambridge International A Level Economics (P3 & P4)
summary: 9708 A Level — allocative and productive efficiency, market failure types, and deadweight loss with GeoGebra.
learningObjectives: Evaluate when government intervention may improve outcomes.

simpleExplanation.title: When markets fall short
simpleExplanation.summary: Allocative efficiency requires MSB = MSC. Market failure occurs when externalities, public goods, or information gaps prevent this.
steps: Productive efficiency: output at minimum ATC.
Allocative efficiency: correct quantity for society.
Public goods: non-rival, non-excludable — free-rider problem.

sections.content: **Market failure** (7.3) links to **externalities** (7.4) and **government policy** (8.1). Use **MSC/MSB** curves — not just supply/demand labels.
